title: pelargonium × mattocksianum
intro: Pelargonium × mattocksianum, also known as Mountain Rose or Mattock's Pelargonium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, specifically a cross between Pelargonium tomentosum and Pelargonium capitatum. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of geraniol, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits associated with Pelargonium × mattocksianum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
